static.description: A polyglot microservices platform for food delivery, built with 5 independent services (API Gateway, Order, Inventory, Driver, Notification) across Kotlin, Java, Python, Go, and TypeScript stacks, communicating via RabbitMQ events and fronted by a JWT-authenticated API Gateway.
domain_concepts: OrderStatus: Enum representing the lifecycle states of an order: PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED, with CANCELLED and REFUNDED as terminal/side states.
OrderItem: An embeddable JPA entity representing a line item within an order, containing menu item ID, name, quantity, and unit price.
MenuItem: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant menu item with stock_quantity (-1 means unlimited) and availability tracking.
Driver: A MongoDB document representing a delivery driver with name, phone, vehicle type, availability status, and GPS location.
GeoPoint: A struct/document embedded in Driver storing latitude and longitude coordinates for real-time location tracking.
JwtAuthFilter: A Spring Cloud Gateway global filter that validates JWT tokens and forwards the verified customer ID as an X-Customer-Id header to downstream services.
acquire_lock: A Redis-based distributed lock function in the Inventory Service that prevents concurrent stock overselling by acquiring an exclusive lock on a menu item.
order.events: The RabbitMQ exchange name used for publishing order lifecycle events that the Notification Service consumes.
stripePaymentIntentId: The Stripe PaymentIntent ID (pi_xxxx) stored on an Order, used for manual capture and refund operations.
Restaurant: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant with name, address, cuisine type, rating, and associated menu items.
isValidTransition: A static method on OrderStatus that enforces the allowed state machine transitions for order lifecycle.

dynamic.install_command: cd order-service && mvn dependency:resolve && cd ../inventory-service && pip install -r requirements.txt && cd ../notification-service && npm install && cd ../driver-service && go mod download && cd ../api-gateway && ./gradlew dependencies
dynamic.architecture_summary: The platform follows a microservices architecture with an API Gateway (Kotlin/Spring Cloud Gateway) handling JWT authentication and routing to downstream services. The Order Service (Java/Spring Boot) manages order lifecycle and Stripe payments, publishing events to RabbitMQ which the Notification Service (Node.js/TypeScript) consumes for SMS/email delivery. The Inventory Service (Python/FastAPI) uses Redis distributed locks for stock reservation, and the Driver Service (Go) tracks driver profiles and locations in MongoDB.

key_components.name: GatewayApplication
key_components.responsibility: Entry point for the Spring Cloud Gateway API Gateway that routes requests to downstream microservices.

key_components.name: JwtAuthFilter
key_components.responsibility: Global gateway filter that validates JWT tokens and propagates the authenticated customer ID to downstream services.

key_components.name: OrderServiceApplication
key_components.responsibility: Entry point for the Order Service Spring Boot application.

key_components.name: OrderController
key_components.responsibility: REST controller exposing endpoints for creating, listing, updating, and cancelling orders.

key_components.name: OrderService
key_components.responsibility: Business logic for order CRUD operations, Stripe payment integration, and RabbitMQ event publishing.

key_components.name: Order
key_components.responsibility: JPA entity representing a customer order with items, status, payment intent, and delivery address.

key_components.name: OrderStatus
key_components.responsibility: Enum defining order lifecycle states and valid state transitions.

key_components.name: inventory-service/main.py
key_components.responsibility: FastAPI application handling restaurant/menu queries and stock reservation with Redis distributed locks.

key_components.name: models.py
key_components.responsibility: SQLAlchemy ORM models for Restaurant and MenuItem entities.

key_components.name: driver-service/main.go
key_components.responsibility: Go HTTP server for driver profile management and location tracking backed by MongoDB.

key_components.name: notification-service/index.ts
key_components.responsibility: Consumes RabbitMQ order events and sends SMS (Twilio) and email (SendGrid) notifications with idempotency protection.

agent_safe_operations: Add new REST endpoints to existing service controllers
Add new unit or integration tests for any service
Modify business logic in OrderService, inventory main.py, or driver main.go
Add new domain models or DTOs within existing service packages
Add new RabbitMQ event consumers or publishers
Refactor existing code within a single service boundary
Add new dependencies to pom.xml, requirements.txt, package.json, or go.mod
Add or update Dockerfiles for individual services
agent_forbidden_operations: Modify .env or .env.example with actual secrets or credentials
Modify the openapi.yaml specification without explicit approval
Change database migration scripts or schema definitions in production
Alter JWT secret handling or authentication bypass logic
Change Stripe API key handling or payment flow without review
Remove or weaken the JwtAuthFilter's security checks
potential_pitfalls: Each service uses a different language and build system — changes must use the correct toolchain (Maven for order-service, pip for inventory-service, go for driver-service, npm for notification-service, Gradle for api-gateway).
The inventory service uses async SQLAlchemy with asyncpg — standard synchronous SQLAlchemy patterns will not work.
Order status transitions are validated by OrderStatus.isValidTransition() — attempting an invalid transition will throw IllegalStateException.
The API Gateway's JwtAuthFilter runs at order -100, before routing — any new public endpoints must be added to the PUBLIC_PATHS list or they will require authentication.
The notification service uses an in-memory Set for idempotency which resets on restart — do not assume persistence across deployments.
Stock reservation in inventory-service requires Redis distributed locks — tests must mock or provide a Redis instance.
The order-service uses Stripe with MANUAL capture method — payments are authorized but not captured until order status progresses.
Docker Compose depends_on with health checks means services may fail if infrastructure containers are not healthy — watch for race conditions in local development.
The driver-service uses raw net/http without a framework — routing is done via manual path/method matching in ServeHTTP.
The api-gateway uses Kotlin with Spring Cloud Gateway (reactive/WebFlux) — blocking calls will cause issues.
